question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /health/readiness endpoint to detect the exact moment when the Rosetta node completes its initial sync (transitions from 503 to 200), and immediately submit a large batch of transactions that exploit any inconsistencies in the node's initial state, causing incorrect balance calculations and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response validation in the checkNetworkStatus function (no check that networkList is non-nil before accessing NetworkIdentifiers) to cause a nil pointer dereference panic when the internal /network/list returns a nil response body, crashing the health check goroutine and potentially the entire service, interrupting fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the health endpoints share the same HTTP server as the transaction API to use HTTP/2 stream multiplexing to interleave health check requests with transaction requests, causing the server to prioritize health check processing and starve transaction requests of CPU time, leading to transaction timeouts and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential nature of the readiness checks (postgresql check runs before network check) to use the response time of /health/readiness as a side channel to determine whether the PostgreSQL check passed or failed independently of the network check, enabling targeted attacks against the specific failing component to prolong the outage and maximize the window of direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the postgres health check DSN (rosettaConfig.Db.GetDsn()) uses sslmode=disable to perform a network-level interception of the health check's PostgreSQL connection, extracting the database credentials and using them to directly manipulate transaction records, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error message format in log.Errorf('Readiness check, /network/list failed: %v', err) to cause log injection by triggering errors with crafted messages that contain control characters or ANSI escape sequences, corrupting the log output and hiding evidence of an ongoing attack that causes direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /health/liveness endpoint to confirm the Rosetta node is alive and then use the node's public API to enumerate all available network identifiers, selecting a target network for a transaction replay attack that causes direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that checkNetworkStatus calls rosettaClient.NetworkAPI.NetworkStatus with the first network identifier from the list (networkList.NetworkIdentifiers[0]) without verifying it matches the expected network, to detect when the node has switched to a different network (e.g., testnet vs mainnet) and exploit the misconfiguration to submit transactions on the wrong network, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request body size limits on the /health/readiness endpoint to send a large request body that causes the health check handler to consume excessive memory while parsing the request, triggering an OOM condition that crashes the service and drops pending transactions, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the health check's rosettaClient uses the 'readiness-check' user agent string to bypass any user-agent-based rate limiting on the internal Rosetta API, allowing the attacker to use the health check as a proxy to make unlimited internal API calls that exhaust resources and prevent transaction processing, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that /health/readiness and /health/liveness are served without TLS to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the health check responses (in a shared network environment), injecting false 'healthy' responses to prevent the orchestrator from restarting a compromised node that is processing fraudulent transactions causing direct loss of funds?
